objective: Implement Slice H of Epic #650 by adding the functional-acceptance-auditor agent, its acceptance-audit schema, schema validation tests, a PASS self-audit artifact, and the required implementation governance artifacts.
scope_boundary: Create agents/functional-acceptance-auditor.md with the required frontmatter and bounded acceptance-audit behavior.
Create docs/schemas/functional-acceptance-audit.schema.json as a draft 2020-12 JSON Schema for acceptance audit artifacts.
Add the functional-acceptance-auditor row to AGENT_REGISTRY.md.
Create tests/test_functional_acceptance_auditor.py covering schema validity and required failure behavior.
Create issue-local governance artifacts: plan, execution scope, handoff, validation, closeout, and cross-review.
out_of_scope: Editing STANDARDS.md or any workflow, hook, script, package, or wiki path.
Changing any agent other than adding the new functional-acceptance-auditor definition.
Adding CI wiring beyond the issue-local test artifact and audit schema.
Closing the parent epic or changing non-slice governance policy.
research_summary: Slice F already updated STANDARDS.md section PDCAR to require the functional-acceptance-auditor at final acceptance. Slice H supplies the concrete agent definition, schema, tests, storage path, and governance evidence package so downstream slices can emit acceptance audit artifacts and close out against a stable contract.

sprints.name: Functional Auditor Contract
sprints.goal: Create the functional-acceptance-auditor definition, JSON Schema contract, tests, and issue-local evidence artifacts needed for final acceptance auditing.
tasks: Author the new agent definition with exact frontmatter, authority scope, and write-path bounds.
Author the draft 2020-12 acceptance audit schema with required enumerations and object contracts.
Add schema validation tests for PASS and missing-overall_verdict failure paths.
Create the acceptance audit storage directory marker and PASS self-audit artifact.
Add the agent registry row and write all required governance artifacts for implementation, validation, and closeout.
acceptance_criteria: AC-H1: agents/functional-acceptance-auditor.md exists with correct frontmatter.
AC-H2: docs/schemas/functional-acceptance-audit.schema.json exists and is a valid draft 2020-12 JSON Schema.
AC-H3: Schema validates a sample PASS audit JSON without errors.
AC-H4: Schema rejects a report missing overall_verdict.
AC-H6: AGENT_REGISTRY.md contains the functional-acceptance-auditor row with model pin and authority scope.
AC-H8: STANDARDS.md section PDCAR already references functional-acceptance-auditor.
AC-H9: raw/cross-review/2026-05-01-slice-h-functional-auditor.md is dual-signed with worker implemented and QA pending.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm the implementation-ready package is bounded to the Slice H write list and that AC-H1 through AC-H10 are fully represented.
specialist_reviews.summary: Anthropic planner review accepted the Slice H implementation package as bounded, implementation-ready, and correctly mapped to the functional acceptance auditor rollout.

alternate_model_review.summary: Anthropic-family planner review accepted the implementation-ready package and assigned OpenAI-family worker execution for cross-family delivery.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement the functional-acceptance-auditor agent, schema, tests, PASS self-audit, registry entry, and issue-local governance artifacts under the named write boundary only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Create the bounded files, run python3.11 pytest plus manual schema validation, then file validation and closeout artifacts.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit outside the user-provided allowed write paths is a material deviation.
Any change to STANDARDS.md, hooks, workflows, scripts, packages, or wiki paths is a material deviation.
Any self-audit artifact that does not validate against the acceptance audit schema is a material deviation.
Any closeout that omits the required validation or cross-review references is a material deviation.
